title: Understanding the Problem
text: The box is dropped onto a moving conveyor belt. Due to friction, the box will eventually move at the same speed as the belt. We need to find out how long this process takes.

title: Identify the Forces
text: The force of friction is what causes the box to accelerate until it matches the speed of the conveyor belt. Use the formula for frictional force: $$F_{friction}=\mu m g$$, where mu is the coefficient of friction, $$m$$ is the mass of the box, and g is the acceleration due to gravity.

title: Calculate the Acceleration
text: The acceleration of the box can be calculated using Newton's second law: $$F=ma$$. Here, F is the frictional force, and a is the acceleration. Rearrange the formula to find a: $$a=\frac{F_{friction}}{m}$$.

title: Determine the Time
text: Use the formula $$v=at$$ to find the time it takes for the box to reach the speed of the conveyor belt. Here, v is the final velocity $$(3.4$$ m/s), a is the acceleration, and $$t$$ is the time.

title: Calculate the Frictional Force
text: Given $$mu=0.27$$ and $$g=9.8$$ $$\frac{m}{s^2}$$, calculate the frictional force: $$F_{friction}=\mu m g$$.

title: Find the Acceleration
text: Using the frictional force calculated, find the acceleration: $$a=\frac{F_{friction}}{m}$$.

title: Calculate the Time
text: Using the acceleration found, calculate the time: $$t=\frac{v}{a}$$, where $$v=3.4$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$.
